addicted.definition: Unable to stop using or doing something, even when it is harmful.
addicted.example: Some people are addicted to their phones.

physically.definition: Related to the body and being near someone.
physically.example: She was physically close but mentally far away.

missing.definition: Not noticing or failing to see.
missing.example: She was missing her baby's signals.

trouble.example: Being ignored will trouble your child.

annoying.definition: Making you feel slightly angry or bothered.
annoying.example: It is annoying when a friend looks at their phone.

personality.definition: The qualities that make someone who they are.
personality.example: A child is still forming their personality.

damage.example: Ignoring your child can damage their sense of self.

forming.definition: In the process of developing or creating.
forming.example: Young children are forming their habits.

habits.definition: Things you do regularly, often without thinking.
habits.example: Good habits are built during childhood.

alcoholics.definition: People who cannot stop drinking alcohol.
alcoholics.example: Alcoholics often struggle to take care of their families.

addicts.definition: People who cannot stop using a harmful substance or doing a harmful activity.
addicts.example: Phone addicts miss important moments with their children.

substance.definition: A material or chemical, especially a drug or alcohol.
substance.example: The substance they are addicted to controls them.

priority.definition: The thing that is most important and needs attention first.
priority.example: Your child should be your top priority.

denied.example: The children were denied the attention they needed.

recommend.definition: To suggest something as a good idea.
recommend.example: I don't recommend using phones near young children.

depriving.definition: Taking something away from someone; not giving them what they need.
depriving.example: You will be depriving them of important contact.

dramatic.definition: Exaggerated or made to seem more serious than it is.
dramatic.example: I don't mean to be dramatic, but this matters.

compulsive.definition: Done because of a strong urge that is hard to control.
compulsive.example: Compulsive phone checking is a common problem.

disconnected.definition: Not connected; feeling separate from others.
disconnected.example: She felt disconnected from the people around her.

haunting.definition: Staying in your mind and causing worry or sadness.
haunting.example: The feeling of emptiness kept haunting her.

replacement.definition: Something used instead of something else.
replacement.example: A screen is not a replacement for human contact.

instant.example: Phones give an instant sense of connection.

meaningful.example: Meaningful contact with others is what we need.

viable.definition: Able to work or be successful.
viable.example: Screen time is not a viable substitute for love.

substitute.definition: Something used in place of another thing.
substitute.example: TV is no substitute for real conversation.

attached.example: Many parents are attached to their phones.

intensely.definition: In a very strong or deep way.
intensely.example: Children need contact more intensely than adults.

develop.definition: To grow and become more complete or advanced.
develop.example: People do not develop normally in isolation.

isolation.definition: Being alone and separated from other people.
isolation.example: Children cannot develop well in isolation.

nanny.definition: A person paid to take care of children in their home.
nanny.example: The nanny also needs to put down her phone.

childminder.definition: A person who is paid to look after children, usually in the childminder's home.
childminder.example: Make sure your childminder limits screen time.

relative.example: A relative who cares for your child should know this too.

staring.definition: Looking at something for a long time without moving your eyes.
staring.example: If you are always staring at a screen, your child will too.

ruined.example: You haven't ruined them — you can still change.

screens.definition: The flat surfaces on phones, computers, and TVs that show images.
screens.example: Too much time on screens is not good for children.

simply.definition: Just; only; in an easy way.
simply.example: Simply stop and make space for your child.
